steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the context of the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension interface.
Type text: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' in the Visual Studio Code command palette input field, located near the top of the interface.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the dropdown menu in the command palette of Visual Studio Code.

Type text: 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' in the Visual Studio Code "Run and Debug" dropdown menu's text input field labeled "Type the name of a launch configuration to run." Select the corresponding option from the list displaying debugger configurations.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option from the dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code debug configuration panel.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field in the Microsoft sign-in page to activate the text entry.
Click the "Close" button (red "x") in the top-right corner of the browser tab to close the Microsoft login page.
Press the F1 key to invoke the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, currently in debugging mode for a Teams application.

Type text 'Microsoft 365 Agents: Create New Agent/App' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette, which is activated and located at the top of the interface, displaying suggestions relevant to debugging and Microsoft 365 Agents commands.
Press "Enter" to confirm the selection of "Microsoft 365 Agents: Create New Agent/App" in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click on the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown list, located in the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" option in the "Choose the folder where your project root folder will be located" prompt within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type text:  ${{var:apps_name:vscuse_app_#####}}  into the input field within the command palette of Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to submit the text input ("test123") in the Visual Studio Code command palette, where the prompt reads "Press 'Enter' to confirm your input or 'Escape' to cancel."
Right-click the "Preview README.md" tab in the Visual Studio Code interface located at the top-center of the application window.
Click on the "Close" option in the context menu, located under the file tab in the Visual Studio Code interface, to close the active file.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code interface while viewing the "Explorer" panel and the "TEST123" workspace.
Type text in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging'.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selected "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type text: 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' in the launch configuration dropdown search box within the Visual Studio Code interface, ensuring the correct debugging configuration is selected.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion 'Port 3978 is occupied' is visible
